name: Jim Carrey
blurb: The physical-impressionist who hammered Yuk Yuk's and the Comedy Store into a one-man rubber-face engine, then weaponized it for In Living Color and the entire mid-1990s box office.
bio: Jim Carrey is the rare comic whose stand-up is mostly back-channel evidence for the larger career: he worked Toronto's Yuk Yuk's as a teenager, killed at the Comedy Store with elastic-face impression sets that nobody else could physically do, debuted on Carson in 1983 with an Elvis bit, and parlayed the impressionist act into a Rodney Dangerfield opener slot and an NBC failed sitcom (The Duck Factory). The stage work was the audition; the payoff arrived with In Living Color in 1990 (Fire Marshall Bill, Vera de Milo), the 1991 Showtime hour Unnatural Act, and the 1994 triple of Ace Ventura / The Mask / Dumb and Dumber that made him the highest-paid actor of his era. He has hosted SNL three times (1996, 2011, 2014) and gives a stand-up energy to talk-show appearances that most actors no longer attempt.

timeline.ev: Born January 17 in Newmarket, Ontario, Canada
First stage appearance at Yuk Yuk's in Toronto at age 15 (bombs)
Returns to Yuk Yuk's and becomes a regular impressionist act
U.S. TV debut on The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson, Nov. 23, doing an Elvis Presley impression
First appearance on Late Night with David Letterman (July 25); The Duck Factory premieres on NBC
Cast on In Living Color; creates Fire Marshall Bill, Vera de Milo, the Sniper
Showtime special Jim Carrey: Unnatural Act airs, dedicated to his late mother Kathleen
Ace Ventura: Pet Detective, The Mask, and Dumb and Dumber all release; becomes the dominant American comedy lead
First time hosting Saturday Night Live (May 18)
Hosts SNL a third time (Oct. 25) with the Helvis monologue

accolades: Two Golden Globe Awards (Best Actor — Drama for The Truman Show, 1999; Best Actor — Drama for Man on the Moon, 2000)
First actor to be paid $20 million for a single role (The Cable Guy, 1996)
MTV Movie Awards Comedic Genius Award (2011)
Five-season cast member, In Living Color (1990–1994)

movies_tv.role: cast member (Fire Marshall Bill, Vera de Milo)

movies_tv.t: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind

bits.sp: The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson

bits.t: Kevin Bacon and Wile E. Coyote Impressions
bits.sp: The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson

ranking_notes: Carrey is a special case: the stand-up record is thin (one 1991 Showtime hour) because the impressionist act translated almost immediately to sketch and film. His 1980s Comedy Store / Carson sets are the load-bearing evidence — particularly the impressions block — and they hold up.

Late Night with Letterman first appearance (1984, ID W-FCx_82csA) is uploaded by a private archivist (Don Giller), not Letterman's official channel — verified embeddable but dropped for failing the official-channel gate. Worth surfacing if a Letterman-official upload appears.
